authoritative_sources.title: MAP Guide Appendix 13: Construction Period
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD administers FHA multifamily mortgage insurance, and Appendix 13 directly governs Contractor's Requisition handling, Form HUD-92448, acceptable work, stored materials, HUD inspector review, ineligible items, change-order referral, and supporting documentation for construction advances.
used_for: eligible and ineligible requisition item boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 92, HOME Investment Partnerships Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 24 CFR Part 92 is the governing HOME regulation. It defines HOME project setup, computerized disbursement, drawdown timing, eligible-cost support, completion, closeout, records, property-standard inspections, labor records, environmental records, and retention duties for federally assisted housing development.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: HOME drawdown, project completion, and recordkeeping control

authoritative_sources.title: CPD-IDIS Reference Manual, Chapter 7: Creating and Approving Drawdowns
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's IDIS manual is the direct public workflow reference for CPD grantee drawdown creation, approval, revocation, cancellation, voucher line items, activity funding, LOCCS status, and completed or rejected draw states.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Community-development voucher and drawdown status handling
used_for: system-of-record expectations for HUD grant draws

authoritative_sources.title: AIA G702-1992 Application and Certificate for Payment and G703-1992 Continuation Sheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIA Contract Documents are recognized construction workflow forms. The G702/G703 source defines contractor payment application, architect certification, contract sum, completed and stored work, retainage, previous payments, change-order summary, current payment requested, and schedule-of-values continuation structure.
used_for: retainage, previous-payment, and change-order summary fields

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200, Financial Management and Federal Payment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 2 CFR Part 200 governs federal award financial management, internal controls, and payment. It is authoritative for source-and-application records, fund control, payment timing, construction reimbursement considerations, withholding limits, and timely contractor payment constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federal award financial-control and payment-timing review

authoritative_sources.title: Davis-Bacon and Related Acts Certified Payroll and WH-347 Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOL WHD administers Davis-Bacon labor standards. Its certified payroll guidance is authoritative for payroll and prevailing-wage evidence that federally assisted housing and community-development construction draws may require before payment routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Labor-standard evidence review and exception routing
used_for: routing to grant compliance or labor standards owner

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. construction-safety regulator. This standard is authoritative for construction safety and inspection duties that cannot be overridden by draw processing when site conditions affect completion posture.

workflow_stages.name: Draw intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, identify the project, property, draw number, funding source, contract form, system of record, requested action, and authority boundary before processing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm approved SOV, budget line map, prior draw ledger, funding-source setup, IDIS or lender references, inspection status, environmental release when applicable, labor-standard applicability, and approval matrix.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Draw package evidence tie-out
workflow_stages.description: Tie requested current-period amounts to completed work, stored materials, approved changes, retainage, prior payments, invoices, payroll support, lien waivers, site evidence, and remaining budget or funding balances.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Hold or return packages with missing inspections, noncompliant work, pending change orders, unsupported stored materials, payroll gaps, IDIS setup issues, conflicting policies, or incomplete closeout evidence.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Approval-ready routing and completion evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce status, summary, completion evidence, and escalation artifacts that route the package to authorized approvers without approving payment, drawdown, retainage release, or final completion.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: AIA G702/G703-style pay application or tenant draw worksheet
HUD-92448 Contractor's Requisition, HUD-92403 advance application, HUD-92464 escrow advance request, or IDIS voucher when applicable
approved schedule of values, budget line map, sources-and-uses schedule, and funding-source allocation
prior draw ledger, retainage schedule, payment history, change-order log, and contingency or reallocation approvals
inspection reports, architect certificates, HUD or lender inspection notes, approved plans, permits, correction notices, site photos, and surveys
stored-material invoices, bills of lading, storage inspection evidence, insurance or bond evidence, and lien or security documentation
certified payroll, prevailing wage certificates, Section 3 or MBE/WBE records, environmental release evidence, and federal cross-cutting compliance records
lien waivers, affidavits, title updates, closeout checklists, certificate of occupancy, punch lists, record documents, and turnover records
systems_of_record: HUD IDIS or LOCCS for applicable CPD/HOME draws
certified payroll or labor compliance system

allowed: Classify draw requests as accepted, blocked, returned for rework, approval-ready for routing, or complete as an evidence package.
Identify missing or conflicting draw artifacts, system records, funding-source prerequisites, inspection evidence, labor records, and closeout evidence.
Prepare execution status updates, exception summaries, completion evidence records, project or field-controls summaries, and escalation notes.
escalate: Funding-source, lender, contract, grant, or tenant policy conflicts that materially change draw disposition
Missing environmental release, labor-standard evidence, IDIS setup, inspection, permit, lien-waiver, or closeout records
Requests that move into legal, lien, design, engineering, inspection, code, safety, grant-compliance, housing-finance, or payment-approval authority
refuse: Approving, certifying, transmitting, funding, paying, or releasing any draw, voucher, loan advance, grant disbursement, or retainage
Marking a final draw or closeout complete without required inspection, CO, punch, record-document, lien, and funding-source evidence
Treating unapproved change work, noncompliant work, missing payroll records, unresolved inspection objections, or unsupported stored materials as payable or complete

domain_failure_modes: processing a housing construction draw as a generic invoice and missing HUD, HOME, lender, labor, environmental, affordability, or IDIS prerequisites
treating stored materials, offsite components, or pending change orders as eligible without required inspections, invoices, security, approval, or holdback evidence
marking a final draw complete while certificate of occupancy, final inspection, punch, lien-release, record-document, or closeout records remain open
using tenant shorthand or portal status to imply payment, loan advance, grant drawdown, or retainage release authority the lane does not have
overstepping into lien law, contract entitlement, grant eligibility, design compliance, code interpretation, licensed inspection, or safety signoff
failing to escalate conflicts between lender instructions, public program requirements, contract documents, and tenant policy

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant contract forms, funding stacks, lender draw instructions, local AHJ rules, lien-waiver requirements, approval thresholds, and grant-specific disbursement permissions vary materially by project.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline defines safe workflow boundaries, but runtime must retrieve tenant project records, funding agreements, jurisdiction rules, and authority matrices before asserting a project-specific draw disposition.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, systems of record, controls, and failure handling rather than generic invoice or project-finance behavior.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because remaining variation is explicitly routed to tenant-supplied runtime facts and authority checks.
